name: query
context: Query the Dojo registry - call discovery, learn, bundle, retrieval, and agent routes from one client helper
info: Wraps the registry server's read-heavy and agent-oriented API surface for agents and tooling. Use this skill when you want to inspect registry metadata, resolve a need, search nodes, retrieve a URI, walk a tree, discover the best read-versus-do path, load learn payloads, fetch bundled skill files, inspect graph context, or call the agent ask and learn endpoints without hard-coding route logic. The helper also passes through agent-oriented ranking controls like `mode` and `executable` so callers can bias for runnable skills or knowledge-heavy answers, and the packaged script now works both as a JavaScript import and a direct CLI entrypoint for external agent runtimes.
body:
# Query the Registry

This skill wraps the server's route map for agents and tooling. It resolves natural-language needs, discovers the best learn-versus-do path, searches nodes, loads URIs, pulls trees, reads knowledge payloads, fetches bundled skill files, and calls the agent-oriented recommendation endpoints without hard-coding the whole server.

The manifest uses an `entry` script because the operation map is large enough to deserve its own file and tests. Use this skill after reading [[dojo/api#response-shapes]] and before building automation that depends on [[dojo/knowledge]], bundled skills, or the publish flow.

sections.title: Operation matrix
sections.body: The client supports `index`, `resolve`, `discover`, `search`, `skill`, `bundle`, `tree`, `learn`, `backlinks`, `graph`, `alias`, `ecosystems`, `agent_ask`, and `agent_learn`. That covers the registry metadata route, the grouped discovery route for learn-versus-do decisions, the bundle export route, the read side of the URI and graph APIs, and the two agent-facing helper endpoints. `resolve` and `search` also accept `mode` (`do`, `learn`, or `all`) plus `executable` when you want the registry to prioritize runnable nodes, while the response payload now echoes `query_variants`, `routes`, and `excerpt` fields for better agent explanations. The CLI also accepts structured JSON input so another agent can call it without rebuilding long shell commands.

sections.title: Resolve versus search
sections.body: Use `resolve` when you want the highest-confidence recommendation for a need, often with `tags`, `type`, `eco`, `mode`, or `executable` filters. Use `search` when you want broader recall plus paging controls like `limit` and `offset`. In both cases, follow the ranked result with `skill`, `tree`, or `learn` before acting.

sections.title: Skill, tree, and learn flow
sections.body: Inspect a chosen URI with `skill`, expand an ecosystem with `tree`, and use `depth` when a shallow tree is enough. Once a node looks relevant, call `learn` to load its long-form payload and optionally pass `section` when you want one addressable section rather than the whole document. Pass `question` to `learn` when you want the server to rank relevant sections for that node.

sections.title: Discover and bundle flow
sections.body: Use `discover` when you want the registry to split the answer into `learn_first`, `then_do`, and `alternatives` in one response. Use `bundle` when you want the source package for a node: manifest, `SKILL.md`, `agents/openai.yaml`, references, scripts, and tests when present. This is the route pair that turns Dojo from a search index into a portable skill registry.

sections.title: Knowledge workflow
sections.body: Use `learn` for the full node payload, `backlinks` for inbound references, `graph` for local neighborhood inspection, `alias` when you need to resolve human phrasing to a canonical URI, and `bundle` when understanding is not enough and you need the packaged skill files. This is the path from discovery to understanding and then reuse.

sections.title: Agent workflows
sections.body: Not every useful route is a GET. `agent_ask` sends a `message` plus optional `agent_context` to get a recommended executable node, while `agent_learn` sends a `question` plus optional `current_context` to get context-heavy answers, excerpts, and `then_do` suggestions. This helper wraps both POST shapes behind the same `operation` switch.

sections.title: Error model
sections.body: The helper does not hide transport failures behind fake success values. Missing required fields fail before the request, and the response object preserves the HTTP `status`, `method`, `url`, and parsed JSON body so callers can make their own retry or fallback decisions. Search-like payloads now also expose `reasons`, `execution`, `knowledge`, and `routes` summaries from the registry so an agent can explain why a node matched, then pivot directly into `learn`, `graph`, or `bundle` without rebuilding paths.
